title: BCA Assay for Protein Quantification
author: Karina L Conkrite
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6n5hdg6

steps.title: Use provided 0.2% BSA for creating a standard curve
steps.instruction: Use provided 0.2% BSA for creating a standard curve: Dilute amount necessary for the assay 1:2 using lysis buffer

steps.title: Label tubes (1
steps.instruction: Label tubes (1.5 mL Eppendorf) for standards. Final Conc ug/mLVolume 0.1% BSAVolume buffer Standard (ug) Volume 0.1% BSA (uL) Volume buffer(uL) 0 0 20 2 2 18 4 4 16 6 6 14 8 8 12 10 10 10 15 15 5 20 20 0

steps.title: Add diluted BSA to standard tubes...
steps.instruction: Add diluted BSA to standard tubes last, before adding working reagent.

steps.title: To each sample and all standards,...
steps.instruction: To each sample and all standards, add 1 mL Working Reagent (comprised of [1 mL Reagent A + 20 uL Reagent B] x number of samples + 10% extra for error). Mix well.

steps.title: Place tubes in 37° C incubator...
steps.instruction: Place tubes in 37° C incubator for 30 minutes to react.

steps.title: Aliquot 200 uL from each tube...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 200 uL from each tube to a well of a clear, 96-well plate. This can be done in triplicate if desired.

steps.title: Read on BCA program on GloMax...
steps.instruction: Read on BCA program on GloMax plate reader at λ = 562 nm

steps.title: Use values from BSA standards to create standard curve
steps.instruction: Use values from BSA standards to create standard curve. Can be done in Excel, Statmost, or GraphPrizm. Seeking R2 value as close to 1 as possible. Ensure all your samples are within the standard curve range. If not, need to be redone.

steps.title: Use the resultant y=mx+b equation to...
steps.instruction: Use the resultant y=mx+b equation to quantify the amount of protein in each sample. Dividing by 3 will give you your protein concentration per uL.

steps.title: Keeping all protein on ice throughout,...
steps.instruction: Keeping all protein on ice throughout, use 3 uL/ sample + 17 uL Lysis Buffer for a total volume of 20 uL.

steps.title: Add appropriate amount of Lysis Buffer...
steps.instruction: Add appropriate amount of Lysis Buffer to all tubes for the standard curve, following chart above.
